definition: Private credit with committed capital on 5-7 year fund lifecycles continues deploying regardless of rate environment, creating a modest structural leak in monetary transmission, though the effect is small: $200-300B/yr is ~5-8% of total non-financial credit creation, implying neutral rate adjustment of only 5-15bp.
evidence: Committed capital with 5-7 year fund lifecycles deploys rate-insensitively
Private credit at $200-300B/yr is ~5-8% of total non-financial credit creation
Original 50-100bp neutral rate claim refuted and revised to 5-15bp
Rate insensitivity cuts both ways: easing also less stimulative
open_questions: Is the bidirectional rate insensitivity net positive or negative for the inflation regime?
How fast is private credit growing as a share of total credit?
